content.subtitle: Use
rows: We use **so ... that** and **such ... that** to express a cause and its result — the first part explains the reason, and the second part shows the consequence.
The film was **so scary** that I couldn't sleep.

content.subtitle: Form
rows: so + adjective + that
The weather was **so hot** that we stayed indoors all day.
so + many/much + noun + that
She has **so many friends** that she never feels lonely.
such + a/an + adjective + noun + that
It was **such a long journey** that we stopped twice.
such + adjective + plural noun + that
They gave **such interesting answers** that the teacher was impressed.
such + a lot of + noun + that
He drinks **such a lot of coffee** that he can't sleep at night.
so + adverb + that
She sings **so beautifully** that everyone stopped to listen.

content.subtitle: Use
rows: We use **too** to say that something is more than necessary or acceptable, with a negative effect.
The coffee is **too hot** to drink right now.
We use **enough** to say that there is the right amount of something, or to say there is not enough.
She isn't **old enough** to get a driving licence.

content.subtitle: Form
rows: too + adjective (+ full infinitive)
The suitcase is **too heavy** to carry.
adjective + enough (+ full infinitive)
Are you **fit enough** to run a marathon?
too + many/much + noun (+ full infinitive)
There are **too many people** in this room to have a quiet conversation.
enough + noun (+ full infinitive)
We have **enough time** to grab a coffee before the meeting.
too + adverb (+ full infinitive)
He spoke **too quickly** for me to take notes.
adverb + enough (+ full infinitive)
She performed **well enough** to get a place in the final.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: **Too** is not the same as **very**. We only use **too** when something causes a problem.
**Enough** always follows the adjective or adverb: I'm not **confident enough** to speak in public.
Both **too** and **enough** can be followed by **for + noun/pronoun**: It's too loud **for** the baby to sleep.

sections.title: A: Complete with so or such
sections.instructions: Write so or such in each gap.

items.sentence: It was ...... a noisy restaurant that we couldn't hear each other.
The story was ...... sad that I nearly cried.
They were ...... helpful neighbours that we thanked them with a card.
He answered ...... quickly that nobody understood him.
There were ...... many mistakes that I had to rewrite the essay.
It was ...... an expensive hotel that we stayed somewhere else.
She had ...... little patience that she left after five minutes.
They made ...... a lot of noise that the neighbours complained.

sections.title: B: Complete with too or enough
sections.instructions: Use too or enough in each sentence.

items.sentence: This jacket is ...... small for me to wear.
The ladder isn't long ...... to reach the roof.
We haven't got ...... plates for all the guests.
You're walking ...... quickly; I can't keep up.
He didn't explain the rules clearly ...... .
There are ...... many adverts on this website.
Is this room quiet ...... for you to study?
They arrived ...... early to enter the building.
The music was **so / such** loud that we left.
This phone is **too / enough** expensive for me.
It was **so / such** a strange dream.
The room was warm **enough / too** to sleep in.
There were **so many / such many** people at the station.
It was **such / so** a busy day that I forgot lunch.
You are speaking **too / enough** fast for me.
He worked hard **too / enough** to pass the exam.

sections.title: D: Rewrite with so/such/too/enough
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
The bag was very full, so I couldn't close it. **TOO**
The bag ...... close.

The singer was very popular, so all the tickets sold out. **SO**
The singer was ...... all the tickets sold out.

The street is not wide enough for two buses. **TOO**
The street ...... for two buses.

It was a very cold morning, so we wore gloves. **SUCH**
It was ...... we wore gloves.

She doesn't speak loudly enough for everyone to hear. **TOO**
She speaks ...... everyone to hear.

There was a lot of rain, so the match was cancelled. **SO**
There was ...... the match was cancelled.

sections.title: E: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct form.

items.sentence: It was so a beautiful painting that everyone stopped to look.
items.answer: It was such a beautiful painting that everyone stopped to look.

items.sentence: He isn't enough strong to move the table.
items.answer: He isn't strong enough to move the table.

items.sentence: There were such many people in the queue.
items.answer: There were so many people in the queue.

items.sentence: The exam was enough difficult to make me nervous.
items.answer: The exam was too difficult to make me nervous.

items.sentence: She played the piano enough well to win the prize.
items.answer: She played the piano well enough to win the prize.

items.sentence: We don't have space enough for another desk.
items.answer: We don't have enough space for another desk.

items.sentence: The box is ...... big to fit in the car.
It was ...... a funny joke that everyone laughed.
You didn't practise ...... to play in the concert.
The hotel was ...... expensive that we booked a hostel.
He spoke ...... quietly to hear.
There was ...... much fog that we couldn't see the road.

sections.title: G: Mixed revision
sections.instructions: Find and correct the mistake in each sentence. Rewrite the full sentence correctly.

items.sentence: The test was such easy that everyone passed.
items.answer: The test was so easy that everyone passed.

items.sentence: It was so a delicious meal that we asked for the recipe.
items.answer: It was such a delicious meal that we asked for the recipe.

items.sentence: I haven't got too money to buy a new bike.
items.answer: I haven't got enough money to buy a new bike.

items.sentence: This tea is so hot to drink.
items.answer: This tea is too hot to drink.

items.sentence: They aren't enough old to watch that film.
items.answer: They aren't old enough to watch that film.

items.sentence: There was so many homework that I stayed up late.
items.answer: There was so much homework that I stayed up late.

items.sentence: The instructions were too confusing for us understand them.
items.answer: The instructions were too confusing for us to understand them.

items.sentence: It was such windy that we couldn't play tennis.
items.answer: It was so windy that we couldn't play tennis.

sections.title: H: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning. Use no more than four words.

items.context: The book was very interesting, so I finished it in one day.
items.sentence: The book was ...... I finished it in one day. **SO**

items.context: The table is very heavy and I can't move it.
items.sentence: The table is ...... to move. **TOO**

items.context: It was a very useful lesson and everyone took notes.
items.sentence: It was ...... everyone took notes. **SUCH**

items.context: He doesn't have the experience for the job.
items.sentence: He isn't ...... for the job. **ENOUGH**

items.context: There were many questions, so the meeting lasted longer.
items.sentence: There were ...... the meeting lasted longer. **SO**

items.context: The room was very small and we couldn't all sit down.
items.sentence: The room was ...... all to sit down. **TOO**
